Off Calendar Aug-14-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ernest Tuttle appearing via zoom for Thomas Husmann (executor).
Michelle Di Prisco appearing via zoom (beneficiary).

Mr. Tuttle stated that the final distribution was filed on Friday. Status on close of estate ordered off calendar.

Judge: Cecilia Castellanos. Clerk: A. Datt. Deputy: Singh. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Jan-09-2023 Continued To Mar-29-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom: Ernest Tuttle, Esq. for Thomas Hulsmann (Executor). Deborah Freeland, Esq. for Michelle DiPrisco. Michelle DiPrisco also appeared via Zoom.

Mr. Tuttle addressed the delay stating that there are two additional assets, that they will need to file an amended inventory and appraisal, that the tax returns need to be prepared, that all of this should be done in the next one to two months, and that they will then file the petition.

At the conclusion of comments, the Court continued the status on close of the estate to March 29, 2023, at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204. The Court noted that it would be looking for either a petition for final distribution or a petition for approval of report on status of administration to be on file.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: Choi. Matter not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Dec-12-2022 Continued To Jan-09-23 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY, Appointment Under IAEA
Off Calendar Nov-22-2021 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances via Zoom:
Ernest Tuttle, Esq. for Thomas Hulsman;
Jeff Loew, Esq. for Michelle DiPrisco;
Tom Healy, Esq. for George Daniel Kirkham.

Mr. Loew states that there is no objection. Mr. Healy states that his client has chosen not to file an objection. The Court inquires about the competing petition for letters of administration. Mr. Healy states that the Court can dismiss his client's petition without prejudice.

There being no objections, the Court granted Thomas Hulsman's Petition For Probate Of Will And For Letters Testamentary. The will dated February 26, 2008 is admitted to probate. Thomas Hulsman is appointed Executor of the estate with full IAEA authority. Bond is not required. Michael Kasolas is appointed probate referee. Status on close of the estate is set for: December 12, 2022, at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Deputy: R. Loo. Matter not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-01-2021 Continued To Oct-27-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Thomas Healy, via ZOOM, for George Kirkham, Petitioner; Deborah Freeland and Jeffrey Loew, via ZOOM, for Michelle DiPrisco, in court, Objector.

The court begins by confirming with counsel that a will has been found. Mr. Loew provides oral testimony regarding the history of the will and on its current location. Mr. Loew also confirms that a petition to probate the will is pending and that the parties have agreed to Andrea Leung's appointment as Executor. The court suggests taking the petition for letters of administration off calendar. Mr. Healy confirms that he has seen a copy of the will but requests that the court continue his client's petition until the petition to probate the will has been filed.

The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to 10/27/2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Vedica Puri. Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-03-2021 Continued To Apr-28-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance via Zoom Video Conference:
Jeffrey Loew for Objector Michelle DiPrisco and specially appearing on behalf of attorney Thomas Healy for George Kirkham.

Mr. Loew stated that he is specially appearing for Thomas Healy who had a medical conflict, that his client intends to file a competing petition, that Mr. Healy will not object as long as they agree to the fiduciary, that parties agree for a private fiduciary to serve, that they found out decedent has another daughter who wasn't served yet and needs to be served.

The petition for letters of administration is continued to April 28, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.
